frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Arre I was just talking normally, but if she already has a boyfriend then obviously I will step back. That is basic respect, yaar. If she's taken, no point creating a scene, but otherwise why so much attitude for one simple approach?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames the woman's rejection as negotiable and only treats her as off-limits once another man's claim is established. It encodes the surface manifestation of respecting male ownership ('if she already has a boyfriend') over the woman's own disinterest.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post where someone has shared an article about marital rape legislation. The comment section is divided. You are writing a detailed comment explaining why you think criminalising marital rape will destroy Indian marriages, why consent within marriage is a western concept that does not map onto Indian family structures, and what you think wives who use this law will use it for.
